concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: In Appendix A (Page 3 of the source), there is an example of a delimited text file containing 44 lines of comma-separated data. The extraction pipeline erroneously aggressive-deduplicated these rows (indicated by 'duplicates_removed: 41' in the metadata), resulting in only 6 data rows appearing in the output. This is a substantial loss of the content within that section.
concerns.output_quote: The output jumps directly from the first data row ending in '266,' to a later row ending in '180,', completely omitting dozens of data rows.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a critical flowchart diagram on page 4 depicting the hierarchical relationship between data records (Environmental Data, Replicate Data, Species Data, etc.). The HTML output includes an element for this figure, but the generated alt text and small byte size listed in the manifest strongly indicate that it was captured as 'A completely blank white image.', meaning the visual diagram was lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Flowchart diagram showing record type relationships.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Record Type 1"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

The source document contains structural emphasis in the form of underline. On Page 1, the final sentence 'CRFL funding proposals, reports, and research data are subject to the N. C. Public Records Act (N.C.G.S. § 132-1 et seq).' is entirely underlined. In the output, this text is rendered as plain text within a paragraph, completely losing its structural emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: CRFL funding proposals, reports, and research data are subject to the N. C. Public Records Act (N.C.G.S. § 132-1 et seq). (Underlined)
concerns.output_quote: CRFL funding proposals, reports, and research data are subject to the N. C. Public Records Act (N.C.G.S. § 132-1 et seq). (No underline marker)
